themes.exampleQuote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
I just wish this place was bigger, gets packed quickly!
The workers are so nice every time I visit!
I asked for a blueberry muffin, received a raisin/walnut muffin, let them know, I had to pay for a new muffin.

notable_quote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers keep returning to the specialty drinks, particularly the strawberry matcha and tiramisu latte, while the recurring friction points are tight square footage, inconsistent order accuracy, and wifi too weak for remote workers.
quarter_narrative: Sixteen Pittsburgh creators filmed Delanie's Coffee Shadyside this quarter and posted 17 TikToks that pulled 66,299 plays, with @stephr0se8 alone accounting for 31,200 of them on a video that framed Delanie's as a can't-miss local stop. Meanwhile, the shop posted three times to Instagram in 30 days, has no TikTok account of its own, and reels output sits at zero. The city is doing the storytelling; Delanie's is largely watching. That gap is exactly what separates a Ones to Watch placement from a higher-tier one, especially in a family led this issue by Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange, whose social momentum is the signal that put them at the top. Closing the distance means converting third-party enthusiasm into owned presence, because the demand signal from creators is already there and the review base, at 82 percent five-star across 104 reviews, supports the climb.
tldr_read: Creator coverage is strong and the conversion path is airtight, but Instagram posts have slowed to a trickle and the shop has no TikTok account of its own. Ones to Watch, #4 in Pittsburgh Coffee Shops.
tldr_meaning: The trajectory stalls here unless the owned social output catches up to the third-party coverage, because the product signal and creator fit are already pointing upward.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Sixteen Creators Filmed It This Quarter. Delanie's Has No TikTok To Show For It.

playbook.headline: Claim a TikTok presence before momentum shifts
playbook.action: Sixteen unique creators posted 17 videos to TikTok this quarter; the shop has no account to collect that attention or redirect it.

playbook.headline: Post reels, not just still frames
playbook.action: Three Instagram posts and zero reels in 30 days leaves a 4.67 percent engagement rate with nowhere to grow against a family median that rewards video.

playbook.headline: Address the order and wifi complaints publicly
playbook.action: Review sentiment sits ten points below the family median; a direct owner response to the muffin-swap and wifi complaints would signal accountability and lift that number.
